div.p: that is done, judgment will be administered in righteousness;1 anarchy and confusion will be destroyed, and “nations will learn war no more.”2 It is for want of this great governing principle, that all this confusion has existed; “for it is not in man that walketh, to direct his steps;”3 this we have fully shown.

If there was anything great or good in the world, it came from God.4 The construction of the first vessel was given to Noah, by revelation.5 The design of the ark was given by God, “a pattern of heavenly things.”6 The learning of the Egyptians, and their knowledge of astronomy was no doubt taught them by Abraham7 and Joseph, as their records testify, who received it from the Lord. The art of working in brass,8 silver, gold, and precious stones, was taught by revelation, in the wilderness.9 The architectural designs of the Temple at Jerusalem,10 together with its ornaments and beauty,11 were given of God. Wisdom to govern the house of Israel was given to Solomon,12 and the Judges of Israel; and if he had always been their king, and they subject to his mandate, and obedient to his laws, they would still have been a great and mighty people—the rulers of the universe, and the wonder of the world.

If Nebuchadnezzar, or Darius, or Cyrus,13 or any other king possessed knowledge or power,14 it was from the same source, as the Scriptures abundantly testify. If, then, God puts up one, and sets down another15 at His pleasure,16 and made instruments of kings, unknown to themselves, to fulfill His prophecies,17 how much more was He able, if man would have been subject to His mandate to regulate the affairs of this world, and promote peace and happiness among the human family!

The Lord has at various times commenced this kind of government, and tendered His services to the human family. He selected Enoch, whom He directed, and gave His law unto,18 and to the people who were with him; and when the world in general would not obey the commands of God,19 after walking with God, he translated Enoch and his church,20 and the Priesthood or government of heaven was taken away.
Abraham was guided in all his family affairs21 by the Lord; was conversed with by angels,22 and by the Lord;23 was told where to go, and when to stop;24 and prospered exceedingly in all that
